high_value_elements: Correctly identifies acupuncture/electroacupuncture mechanism: endogenous opioid release, spinal cord modulation, serotonin increase, bradykinin decrease
Correctly describes PBM mechanism at the cellular level (cytochrome C, ATP, nitric oxide, ROS) as a non-pharmacological pain intervention
Identifies weight management as reducing both mechanical joint load AND inflammatory adipokine burden
Correctly identifies omega-3 fatty acids as modulating the arachidonic acid pathway toward pro-resolving mediators
Describes therapeutic exercise as producing analgesia via endorphin release and improved joint stability
major_concerns: Cannot describe any mechanism for any non-pharmacological modality
States non-pharmacological modalities have no evidence base
Cannot distinguish electroacupuncture from dry needling in mechanism or indication

high_value_elements: Constructs a realistic home plan (specific exercises, environmental modifications, dietary changes) for an overweight OA Labrador
Identifies underwater treadmill as appropriate low-impact exercise reducing joint load while maintaining muscle strengthening
Applies species-appropriate non-pharmacological strategies for at least one non-canine species
Sets a measurable outcome (e.g., 10% body weight reduction, improved pain scale score) for the plan
Identifies the clinical threshold at which non-pharmacological management alone is insufficient and escalation is warranted
major_concerns: Recommends high-impact exercise for a dog with severe bilateral OA
Constructs a plan with no measurable outcomes and no re-evaluation trigger
Cannot apply any species-specific environmental modification for any non-canine species

high_value_elements: Identifies PBM over confirmed neoplasia as an absolute contraindication and declines treatment with client explanation
Recognizes when non-pharmacological management alone is insufficient based on escalating pain score despite full non-pharm delivery
Prioritizes patient safety over owner preference when a non-pharm modality is contraindicated
Identifies when a non-pharmacological modality may delay necessary pharmacologic or surgical management
major_concerns: Agrees to laser a dog with confirmed splenic mass without raising the contraindication
Has no escalation threshold and treats non-pharmacological plan as always sufficient
Cannot identify any scenario where non-pharmacological modalities are contraindicated or insufficient

high_value_elements: Explains the adipokine-inflammation connection when justifying weight management as a pain intervention
Applies gate control theory basis for TENS and physical therapy
Provides mechanism-based rationale for electroacupuncture over dry needling for a neurological patient
Justifies underwater treadmill over land-based exercise with specific biomechanical rationale
major_concerns: Recommends non-pharmacological modalities without any mechanism-based justification
Cannot connect a modality's mechanism to the specific patient's condition
Relies entirely on personal experience without any mechanism-based support

high_value_elements: Acknowledges that evidence quality varies across non-pharmacological modalities and identifies which have strongest support
Identifies personal training gaps in specific modalities before offering them
Recognizes when complex rehabilitation cases should be referred to a certified rehabilitation therapist
Acknowledges uncertainty about nutraceutical dosing optimization in specific species
major_concerns: Presents all non-pharmacological modalities as equally evidence-based
Has no referral threshold for rehabilitation cases beyond personal competence
Cannot identify any species for which they lack sufficient training in non-pharmacological management

high_value_elements: Discusses the synergism between PBM and electroacupuncture with mechanistic overlap
Integrates non-pharmacological and pharmacological modalities into a unified plan addressing mechanism, dose reduction, and monitoring
References specific PBM research (DM rehabilitation study, tendinopathy studies) when supporting clinical recommendations
Discusses practice systems challenges of delivering non-pharmacological modalities consistently
major_concerns: Cannot integrate non-pharmacological and pharmacological modalities into a single coherent plan
Treats non-pharmacological modalities as entirely separate from pharmacological management
Lists modalities without understanding when, why, or how to apply them to specific patients
